danger_signals.visual_form: Map interface showing enemy positions and movement vectors from empire map
danger_signals.meaning: Military assignment active; player must defend city or suffer damage to peace rating

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated flame graphics spreading across buildings in isometric view
danger_signals.meaning: Hazard event occurring; uncontrolled fire reduces housing and prosperity

danger_signals.visual_form: Building destruction and terrain disruption visualized in city map
danger_signals.meaning: Catastrophic world event; structural integrity compromised, recovery required

danger_signals.visual_form: Advisor alerts and visual indicators in affected districts
danger_signals.meaning: Peace rating threatened; social stability degraded

danger_signals.visual_form: Housing quality degradation visuals, advisor warnings
danger_signals.meaning: Unmet citizen needs; culture, prosperity, or peace ratings declining

safety_signals.visual_form: Mission selection screen designation; no enemy units on map
safety_signals.meaning: Military threats absent; focus on city development and natural disasters only

safety_signals.visual_form: Culture buildings (temples, theaters, schools) distributed across map
safety_signals.meaning: Cultural needs met; contributes to overall prosperity and citizen satisfaction

safety_signals.visual_form: Housing structures with visual indicators of quality/upgrades
safety_signals.meaning: Citizens' needs being satisfied; population stability and growth supported

safety_signals.visual_form: Advisor confirmations of timely tribute payments and gift deliveries
safety_signals.meaning: Caesar's political support maintained; no penalty threats

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Caesar as political authority; Roman city architecture
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Roman-style buildings (forums, coliseums, aqueducts); structured grid-based city layout; historical Roman city names (Brundisium, Londinium)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Variable based on scenario (selectable in map editor)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Different unit sprites and architectural styles depending on enemy faction identity
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Siege camps; fortification positions; units approaching from map edges

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy-free map; no hostile units visible; focus on city infrastructure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Citizens move freely; walkers distribute resources without military interference; construction and trade proceed unimpeded

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy units visible on map; defensive fortifications required; military buildings constructed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Military units respond to threats; workers may flee danger zones; resource distribution affected by combat zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Building destruction; terrain alterations; smoke and destruction effects in isometric view
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Population flees damaged areas; recovery construction begins; economic disruption evident in advisor reports

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Well-maintained buildings; active cultural structures; bustling city atmosphere
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Population growth increases; walker density increases; positive morale indicators

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual indicators of civil unrest; possible building damage from riots
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Citizens avoid affected areas; advisor alerts; potential population emigration

environmental_storytelling.example: Different map regions generate specific resources (river channels, fertile land, mining areas); city placement and economic strategy directly reflect geographic constraints of historical locations
Assignments set in actual Roman cities (Brundisium, Londinium); environment reflects historical context and expected challenges of that era/location
Citizens walking past buildings to receive services; visual representation of supply chains and service coverage through movement patterns and behavior
Culture buildings (temples, theaters) placed to demonstrate religious/entertainment infrastructure; housing clusters reflect urban planning philosophy

environmental_storytelling.visual_manifestation: Two-dimensional isometric view provides clear visibility of entire city state; allows player to assess danger, prosperity, and faction presence at a glance

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Traverses predetermined paths from buildings; delivers services to nearby structures; appears/disappears based on supply availability and building activation
Moves across map toward city; visible on empire map before reaching city map; triggers peace rating threats
Spreads across connected buildings; animation shows progression; can be contained by fire-suppression infrastructure
Stays in homes; contributes to population growth; walker traffic increased as needs met
Emigrates from city; reduced visual activity; advisor alerts triggered

notes: Caesar III communicates world-state primarily through isometric perspective visualization and advisor interface integration. Danger signals rely on event-driven graphics (fire, destruction) and map interface threat indicators. Faction identity is implicit through architectural style and enemy unit differentiation. Political situation (favor, peace) manifests through UI ratings and advisor alerts rather than primarily through world visualization. The walker mechanic serves as a core manifestation system for city service delivery state.
